text: Agile methodology is a popular approach in software development because it focuses on delivering value quickly. Work is divided into short periods called sprints, usually lasting two weeks. Each sprint begins with planning, where the team selects tasks from a list of requirements called a backlog. These tasks are written as user stories, which describe a feature from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team holds brief standup meetings to share progress and identify obstacles. After each sprint, the team reviews their work and adapts the plan for the next sprint. This iterative development process allows for continuous improvement and faster responses to change.
